CVE-2019-13464.title: An issue was discovered in OWASP ModSecurity Core Rule Set (CRS)  ...
CVE-2019-13464.summary: An issue was discovered in OWASP ModSecurity Core Rule Set (CRS) 3.0.2. Use of X.Filename instead of X_Filename can bypass some PHP Script Uploads rules, because PHP automatically transforms dots into underscores in certain contexts where dots are invalid.

CVE-2019-19886.title: Trustwave ModSecurity 3.0.0 through 3.0.3 allows an attacker to s ...
CVE-2019-19886.summary: Trustwave ModSecurity 3.0.0 through 3.0.3 allows an attacker to send crafted requests that may, when sent quickly in large volumes, lead to the server becoming slow or unresponsive (Denial of Service) because of a flaw in Transaction::addRequestHeader in transaction.cc.

CVE-2019-25043.title: ModSecurity 3.x before 3.0.4 mishandles key-value pair parsing, a ...
CVE-2019-25043.summary: ModSecurity 3.x before 3.0.4 mishandles key-value pair parsing, as demonstrated by a "string index out of range" error and worker-process crash for a "Cookie: =abc" header.

CVE-2021-42717.title: ModSecurity 3.x through 3.0.5 mishandles excessively nested JSON  ...
CVE-2021-42717.summary: ModSecurity 3.x through 3.0.5 mishandles excessively nested JSON objects. Crafted JSON objects with nesting tens-of-thousands deep could result in the web server being unable to service legitimate requests. Even a moderately large (e.g., 300KB) HTTP request can occupy one of the limited NGINX worker processes for minutes and consume almost all of the available CPU on the machine. Modsecurity 2 is similarly vulnerable: the affected versions include 2.8.0 through 2.9.4.

CVE-2022-48279.title: In ModSecurity before 2.9.6 and 3.x before 3.0.8, HTTP multipart  ...
CVE-2022-48279.summary: In ModSecurity before 2.9.6 and 3.x before 3.0.8, HTTP multipart requests were incorrectly parsed and could bypass the Web Application Firewall. NOTE: this is related to CVE-2022-39956 but can be considered independent changes to the ModSecurity (C language) codebase.

CVE-2023-28882.title: Trustwave ModSecurity 3.0.5 through 3.0.8 before 3.0.9 allows a d ...
CVE-2023-28882.summary: Trustwave ModSecurity 3.0.5 through 3.0.8 before 3.0.9 allows a denial of service (worker crash and unresponsiveness) because some inputs cause a segfault in the Transaction class for some configurations.

CVE-2023-38285.title: Trustwave ModSecurity 3.x before 3.0.10 has Inefficient Algorithm ...
CVE-2023-38285.summary: Trustwave ModSecurity 3.x before 3.0.10 has Inefficient Algorithmic Complexity.

CVE-2024-1019.title: ModSecurity / libModSecurity 3.0.0 to 3.0.11 is affected by a WAF ...
CVE-2024-1019.summary: ModSecurity / libModSecurity 3.0.0 to 3.0.11 is affected by a WAF bypass for path-based payloads submitted via specially crafted request URLs. ModSecurity v3 decodes percent-encoded characters present in request URLs before it separates the URL path component from the optional query string component. This results in an impedance mismatch versus RFC compliant back-end applications. The vulnerability hides an attack payload in the path component of the URL from WAF rules inspecting it. A back-end may be vulnerable if it uses the path component of request URLs to construct queries. Integrators and users are advised to upgrade to 3.0.12. The ModSecurity v2 release line is not affected by this vulnerability.

CVE-2024-46292.title: A buffer overflow in modsecurity v3.0.12 allows attackers to caus ...
CVE-2024-46292.summary: A buffer overflow in modsecurity v3.0.12 allows attackers to cause a Denial of Service (DoS) via a crafted input inserted into the name parameter. NOTE: this is disputed by the Supplier because it cannot be reproduced. Also, the product's documentation indicates that it is not guaranteed to be usable with very large values of SecRequestBodyNoFilesLimit (which are required by the claimed issue).

CVE-2025-27110.title: Libmodsecurity is one component of the ModSecurity v3 project. Th ...
CVE-2025-27110.summary: Libmodsecurity is one component of the ModSecurity v3 project. The library codebase serves as an interface to ModSecurity Connectors taking in web traffic and applying traditional ModSecurity processing. A bug that exists only in Libmodsecurity3 version 3.0.13 means that, in 3.0.13, Libmodsecurity3 can't decode encoded HTML entities if they contains leading zeroes. Version 3.0.14 contains a fix. No known workarounds are available.

CVE-2019-13464.title: An issue was discovered in OWASP ModSecurity Core Rule Set (CRS)  ...
CVE-2019-13464.summary: An issue was discovered in OWASP ModSecurity Core Rule Set (CRS) 3.0.2. Use of X.Filename instead of X_Filename can bypass some PHP Script Uploads rules, because PHP automatically transforms dots into underscores in certain contexts where dots are invalid.

CVE-2020-15598.title: Trustwave ModSecurity 3.x through 3.0.4 allows denial of service  ...
CVE-2020-15598.summary: Trustwave ModSecurity 3.x through 3.0.4 allows denial of service via a special request. NOTE: The discoverer reports "Trustwave has signaled they are disputing our claims." The CVE suggests that there is a security issue with how ModSecurity handles regular expressions that can result in a Denial of Service condition. The vendor does not consider this as a security issue because1) there is no default configuration issue here. An attacker would need to know that a rule using a potentially problematic regular expression was in place, 2) the attacker would need to know the basic nature of the regular expression itself to exploit any resource issues. It's well known that regular expression usage can be taxing on system resources regardless of the use case. It is up to the administrator to decide on when it is appropriate to trade resources for potential security benefit
